docs.title: FrostWatch
docs.text:
AI-powered cost and query observability for Snowflake — open source and self-hostable.

FrostWatch connects to your Snowflake account, pulls from SNOWFLAKE.ACCOUNT_USAGE, and gives you a dark-themed web dashboard with:

  • 📊 Cost breakdown by warehouse, user, and query tag
  • 🚨 Anomaly detection — spend spikes vs. a 21-day rolling baseline per warehouse
  • 🤖 AI explanations — plain-English summaries for each anomaly (BYO LLM: Claude, GPT-4o, Gemini, or local Ollama)
  • 📦 dbt model costs — per-model credit and performance breakdown parsed from query_tag
  • 📬 Weekly digest delivered to Slack or email
  • 🔌 REST API for integration with your own tooling

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
pipDockerdocker compose 
pip install frostwatch
frostwatch config init    # creates ~/.frostwatch/config.yaml
frostwatch serve          # starts on http://localhost:8000

docker run -p 8000:8000 \
  -e FROSTWATCH_SNOWFLAKE_ACCOUNT=xy12345.us-east-1 \
  -e FROSTWATCH_SNOWFLAKE_USER=myuser \
  -e FROSTWATCH_SNOWFLAKE_PASSWORD=mypass \
  -e FROSTWATCH_LLM_API_KEY=sk-ant-... \
  ghcr.io/arunrajiah/frostwatch

curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/arunrajiah/frostwatch/main/docker-compose.yml
# Edit docker-compose.yml to set your env vars
docker compose up

Then open http://localhost:8000.

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• GitHub
  • PyPI
  • Docker Image

docs.title: REST API Reference
docs.text: FrostWatch exposes a REST API at /api. The interactive Swagger UI is available at http://localhost:8000/docs.

docs.title: GET /api/dashboard
docs.text:
Returns the 7-day and 30-day summary used by the dashboard page.

Response

{
  "total_credits_7d": 12.34,
  "total_cost_7d": 4.11,
  "total_credits_30d": 55.2,
  "total_cost_30d": 18.4,
  "top_warehouses": [
    { "name": "COMPUTE_WH", "credits": 40.1, "cost_usd": 13.37, "pct_of_total": 72.6 }
  ],
  "top_users": [...],
  "recent_anomalies": [...],
  "last_synced": "2026-04-28T08:00:00Z",
  "query_count_7d": 1240
}

docs.title: GET /api/queries
docs.text:
Returns the most expensive queries ordered by credits consumed.

Parameter Type Default Description days int 7 Lookback window (1–365) limit int 50 Max rows returned (1–500)

Response — array of QueryRecord:

[
  {
    "query_id": "01b2c3d4-...",
    "warehouse_name": "COMPUTE_WH",
    "user_name": "TRANSFORMER",
    "role_name": "SYSADMIN",
    "execution_time_ms": 4521.0,
    "bytes_scanned": 1073741824,
    "credits_used": 0.000832,
    "start_time": "2026-04-27T14:22:01Z",
    "end_time": "2026-04-27T14:22:05Z",
    "query_text_preview": "SELECT * FROM ...",
    "query_tag": "{\"app\":\"dbt\",\"node_id\":\"model.proj.orders\"}",
    "dbt_model": "orders",
    "status": "SUCCESS"
  }
]

docs.title: GET /api/warehouses
docs.text:
Returns credit and query aggregates per warehouse.

Parameter Type Default Description days int 30 Lookback window (1–365)

docs.title: GET /api/warehouses/timeseries
docs.text:
Returns daily credit usage per warehouse.

Parameter Type Default Description days int 30 Lookback window (1–365) warehouse string — Filter to a single warehouse

docs.title: GET /api/dbt
docs.text:
Returns credit and performance breakdown by dbt model name.

Parameter Type Default Description days int 30 Lookback window (1–365)

Response — array of DbtModelAgg, sorted by total_credits descending:

[
  {
    "dbt_model": "orders",
    "total_credits": 0.0412,
    "total_cost_usd": 0.0137,
    "query_count": 48,
    "avg_execution_ms": 3210.5
  }
]

docs.title: GET /api/anomalies
docs.text:
Returns detected spend anomalies with optional LLM explanations.

Parameter Type Default Description days int 30 Lookback window (1–365)

docs.title: GET /api/reports
docs.text: Returns previously generated weekly digest reports.

docs.title: POST /api/reports/generate
docs.text: Triggers an on-demand digest report generation.

docs.title: POST /api/sync
docs.text:
Triggers a Snowflake data sync in the background. Rate-limited to 10 requests/minute.

Response: {"status": "started"}

docs.title: GET /api/sync/status
docs.text:
Returns the status of the most recent sync run.

{
  "status": "idle",
  "last_run_at": "2026-04-28T08:00:00Z",
  "last_error": null,
  "rows_synced": 482
}

docs.title: GET /api/settings
docs.text: Returns the current configuration (credentials are redacted — only _set booleans are returned for secrets).

docs.title: PUT /api/settings
docs.text: Updates configuration fields. The server restarts the LLM provider and scheduler on success.

docs.title: POST /api/settings/test-snowflake
docs.text: Tests the current Snowflake connection by running SELECT 1.

docs.title: POST /api/settings/test-email
docs.text: Tests the current SMTP configuration by connecting and issuing EHLO.

docs.title: GET /api/scheduler/jobs
docs.text: Returns the list of scheduled APScheduler jobs (sync cron and report cron).

docs.title: POST /api/scheduler/trigger
docs.text: Manually triggers the report generation job.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: FrostWatch is configured via ~/.frostwatch/config.yaml. Every field can also be set via an environment variable prefixed FROSTWATCH_ — environment variables take precedence over the YAML file.

docs.title: Full reference
docs.text:
# ── Snowflake connection ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
snowflake_account: "xy12345.us-east-1"     # Your Snowflake account identifier
snowflake_user: "your_username"
snowflake_password: "your_password"        # Or FROSTWATCH_SNOWFLAKE_PASSWORD
snowflake_warehouse: "COMPUTE_WH"
snowflake_database: "SNOWFLAKE"            # Built-in SNOWFLAKE database
snowflake_schema: "ACCOUNT_USAGE"          # Leave as-is
snowflake_role: "ACCOUNTADMIN"             # Needs SELECT on ACCOUNT_USAGE

# ── LLM configuration ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Supported providers: anthropic | openai | gemini | ollama
llm_provider: "anthropic"
llm_model: ""                              # Leave empty to use provider default
llm_api_key: "sk-ant-..."                  # Or FROSTWATCH_LLM_API_KEY
llm_base_url: "http://localhost:11434"     # Only used for ollama provider

# ── Alerts ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
slack_webhook_url: ""                      # https://hooks.slack.com/services/...
email_smtp_host: ""                        # e.g. smtp.gmail.com
email_smtp_port: 587
email_smtp_user: ""
email_smtp_password: ""
email_recipients:
  - "data-team@yourcompany.com"

# ── Scheduling ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
schedule_cron: "0 8 * * 1"    # Weekly digest (default: Monday 8am)
sync_cron: "0 */6 * * *"      # Data sync (default: every 6 hours)
snowflake_query_limit: 500    # Max queries fetched per sync

# ── Anomaly detection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
alert_threshold_multiplier: 3.0  # Flag spend > 3× rolling baseline

# ── Cost ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
credits_per_dollar: 3.0           # Adjust for your contract rate

# ── Server ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cors_origins:
  - "http://localhost:5173"
  - "http://127.0.0.1:5173"
  # Add your deployment URL for production:
  # - "https://frostwatch.yourcompany.com"

# ── Storage ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
data_dir: "~/.frostwatch"         # SQLite database and logs location

docs.title: LLM providers
docs.text:
Provider llm_provider Default model Notes Anthropic anthropic claude-sonnet-4-6 Recommended OpenAI openai gpt-4o Google Gemini gemini gemini-2.0-flash Ollama (local) ollama llama3 Set llm_base_url to your Ollama endpoint

LLM is only used to generate anomaly explanations. FrostWatch functions without it — anomalies will still be detected, just without plain-English summaries.

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text:
All config fields are available as env vars by uppercasing the field name and adding the FROSTWATCH_ prefix:

export FROSTWATCH_SNOWFLAKE_ACCOUNT=xy12345.us-east-1
export FROSTWATCH_SNOWFLAKE_PASSWORD=secret
export FROSTWATCH_LLM_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

This is the recommended approach for Docker and Kubernetes deployments.

docs.title: Production deployment
docs.text:
For production add your deployment URL to cors_origins:

cors_origins:
  - "https://frostwatch.yourcompany.com"

Or via env var (comma-separated):

FROSTWATCH_CORS_ORIGINS=https://frostwatch.yourcompany.com

docs.title: dbt Integration
docs.text: FrostWatch automatically detects dbt model costs without any extra configuration. It parses the query_tag field that dbt sets on every Snowflake query and groups credits and performance metrics by model name.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
When dbt runs a model against Snowflake, it sets the QUERY_TAG session parameter to a JSON string such as:

{
  "app": "dbt",
  "dbt_version": "1.8.0",
  "profile_name": "my_project",
  "target_name": "prod",
  "node_id": "model.my_project.orders"
}

FrostWatch parses this tag during each sync and extracts the model name from node_id. Both the flat format above and the nested dbt_snowflake_query_tags wrapper are supported.

docs.title: Enabling query tags in dbt
docs.text:
dbt sets query_tag automatically for Snowflake connections. Verify your profiles.yml includes the Snowflake adapter:

my_project:
  target: prod
  outputs:
    prod:
      type: snowflake
      account: "{{ env_var('SNOWFLAKE_ACCOUNT') }}"
      user: "{{ env_var('SNOWFLAKE_USER') }}"
      password: "{{ env_var('SNOWFLAKE_PASSWORD') }}"
      role: TRANSFORMER
      database: ANALYTICS
      warehouse: TRANSFORM_WH
      schema: dbt_prod
      threads: 4
      # query_tag is set automatically by dbt-snowflake >= 1.0

No additional configuration is needed — dbt-snowflake sets query_tag on every query by default from version 1.0 onwards.

docs.title: The dbt Models page
docs.text:
Once FrostWatch has synced data containing dbt query tags, the dbt Models page (sidebar → Package icon) shows:

  • Models detected — total number of distinct dbt models observed
  • Total credits — Snowflake credits consumed across all dbt models
  • Total queries — number of dbt model runs
  • Bar chart — top 15 models by credit consumption
  • Detail table — all models with credits, USD cost, query count, and average execution time

docs.title: Backfilling existing data
docs.text:
After upgrading to a FrostWatch version with dbt support, trigger a manual sync to backfill dbt_model for any queries already in the local database:

frostwatch sync
# or click Sync Now in the sidebar

The sync upsert updates the dbt_model field on every re-synced query, so existing rows will be enriched automatically.

docs.title: Supported node types
docs.text: Only model nodes are tracked. Seeds (seed.*), snapshots (snapshot.*), tests (test.*), and other node types are ignored and counted as non-dbt queries.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 or later
  • A Snowflake account with SELECT on SNOWFLAKE.ACCOUNT_USAGE (see Permissions)

docs.title: Install via pip
docs.text: pip install frostwatch

docs.title: Install via Docker
docs.text: docker pull ghcr.io/arunrajiah/frostwatch:latest

docs.title: Install from source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/arunrajiah/frostwatch.git
cd frostwatch
pip install -e ".[dev]"

# Build the frontend (optional — only needed if developing the UI)
cd frontend && npm install && npm run build && cd ..

docs.title: First-time setup
docs.text:
frostwatch config init   # creates ~/.frostwatch/config.yaml with defaults

Edit ~/.frostwatch/config.yaml and fill in your Snowflake credentials and LLM API key, then:

frostwatch serve         # API + UI on http://localhost:8000

On first start FrostWatch will not have any data — click Sync Now in the sidebar to pull the last 30 days of query history from Snowflake.

docs.title: Snowflake permissions
docs.text:
FrostWatch only needs read access to the ACCOUNT_USAGE schema. Create a dedicated role:

CREATE ROLE frostwatch;
GRANT IMPORTED PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE SNOWFLAKE TO ROLE frostwatch;
GRANT ROLE frostwatch TO USER my_service_user;

Then set snowflake_role: frostwatch in your config.

docs.title: CLI reference
docs.text: Command Description frostwatch serve Start the API server (default port 8000) frostwatch sync Run a one-off Snowflake data sync frostwatch config init Create default config file frostwatch config show Print current resolved config frostwatch version Print installed version
